conjecture_title: Informal Conjecture: probabilistic methods insufficient for the $\Delta+2$ bound
paper_title: Progress on the adjacent vertex distinguishing edge colouring conjecture
summary: This is an informal meta-conjecture by Joret and Lochet that probabilistic methods such as the Lovász Local Lemma or entropy compression will not suffice on their own to prove the $\Delta+2$ bound for adjacent vertex distinguishing edge colouring. As of 2026, the full $\Delta+2$ conjecture of Zhang, Liu, and Wang remains open, with the best known general upper bound still being $\Delta+19$ established in the source paper itself. No subsequent work has achieved $\Delta+2$ by any method (which would have forced a reassessment of the conjecture's plausibility), and no formal analysis has appeared confirming or refuting the insufficiency claim about probabilistic techniques.

adjacent vertex distinguishing edge coloring Delta+3 OR Delta+2 proof 2023 2024 2025 arXiv graph theory
Przybylo adjacent vertex distinguishing edge coloring 2022 2023 2024 arXiv improved bound
notes: This conjecture is informal and not mathematically falsifiable in the usual sense — it would be 'disproved' only if someone produced a proof of the $\Delta+2$ bound using purely probabilistic methods. No such proof exists as of the review date. The status 'open' reflects that the underlying $\Delta+2$ conjecture is unresolved and no follow-up paper has addressed the probabilistic-methods limitation claim directly. The best general bound remains $\Delta+19$ (Joret–Lochet 2020, the source paper). A December 2025 arXiv paper (2512.10827) addresses vertex-distinguishing edge coloring but is a distinct problem.
